methods.description: Method for assessing the shapes of unhydrated pollen grains using glycerol and compound microscopy with Nomarski optics.

steps.description: Shapes of unhydrated pollen were assessed by placing pollen grains in 100% glycerol.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare pollen sample by placing unhydrated pollen grains in 100% glycerol
outputs: Pollen sample in glycerol on a slide

steps.description: examining with a 1003 objective on a Zeiss Axioskop compound microscope (Nomarski optics).
steps.searchable_summary: Examine and image pollen sample using a compound microscope with 100x objective and Nomarski optics

steps.description: Multiple pollen grains were observed for each line (n $ 20).
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and record data for at least 20 pollen grains per genetic line

steps.description: For lines exhibiting interesting abnormal exine phenotypes, pollen from multiple flowers (and multiple plants, after the lines were established) was assessed.
steps.searchable_summary: Increase sampling size and source (multiple flowers/plants) for lines showing abnormal phenotypes
outputs: Increased observation data for specific lines

methods.method_name: Visualization of Exine Structure and Thickness (Auramine O & LSCM)
methods.description: Method for visualizing pollen exine structure and determining exine thickness using auramine O staining and laser scanning confocal microscopy (LSCM).

steps.description: To visualize the exine structure, pollen grains were removed from anthers onto a microscope slide (by gently brushing an anther on a slide or by removing pollen with thin forceps).
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer pollen grains from anthers onto a microscope slide
outputs: Pollen grains on a microscope slide

steps.description: Auramine O was dissolved in 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, and diluted to 0.001% in 17% Suc.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare 0.001% Auramine O staining solution in 17% Sucrose from a stock dissolved in 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5
outputs: 0.001% Auramine O staining solution in 17% Sucrose

steps.description: a small drop of auramine O solution was added to pollen. Pollen grains were simultaneously hydrated and stained with auramine O
steps.searchable_summary: Add auramine O staining solution to pollen grains on the slide to simultaneously hydrate and stain them
inputs: Pollen grains on a microscope slide
outputs: Hydrated and stained pollen sample on a slide

steps.description: then examined with a laser scanning confocal microscope (Olympus Fluoview FV1000, 1003 objective, fluorescein isothiocyanate settings).
steps.searchable_summary: Examine and image stained pollen using LSCM with 100x objective and FITC settings
inputs: Laser scanning confocal microscope (Olympus Fluoview FV1000)
outputs: Confocal microscopic images of pollen exine surface

steps.description: In addition to images of the exine surface, we took optical sections by LSCM through the center of pollen grains to determine exine thickness.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire optical sections through the center of pollen grains using LSCM and measure exine thickness
inputs: LSCM software for optical sectioning and measurement

steps.description: Multiple pollen grains were observed for each line (n $ 20).
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and record LSCM data for at least 20 pollen grains per genetic line
outputs: Quantitative LSCM observation data (n>=20 per line)

steps.description: For lines exhibiting interesting abnormal exine phenotypes, pollen from multiple flowers (and multiple plants, after the lines were established) was assessed.
steps.searchable_summary: Increase LSCM sampling size and source (multiple flowers/plants) for lines showing abnormal exine phenotypes
outputs: Increased LSCM observation data for specific lines

methods.description: Scanning electron microscopy was performed as described in a previous publication (Dobritsa et al., 2009b). Used for high-resolution imaging of surface structures.

steps.description: Scanning electron microscopy... were performed as described (Dobritsa etren al., 2009b).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform scanning electron microscopy according to the protocol described in Dobritsa et al., 2009b

methods.description: Transmission electron microscopy was performed as described in a previous publication (Dobritsa et al., 2009b). Used for high-resolution imaging of internal structures.

steps.description: ...TEM... were performed as described (Dobritsa et al., 2009b).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform transmission electron microscopy according to the protocol described in Dobritsa et al., 2009b

methods.description: Acetolysis was performed as described in a previous publication (Dobritsa et al., 2009b). This chemical treatment removes cytoplasm and intine, leaving primarily the exine.

steps.description: ...acetolysis were performed as described (Dobritsa et al., 2009b).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform acetolysis chemical treatment according to the protocol described in Dobritsa et al., 2009b

methods.method_name: Anther Sectioning and Toluidine Blue Staining
methods.description: Sectioning of developing anthers and subsequent toluidine blue staining were performed as described in a previous publication (Dobritsa et al., 2010). Used for visualizing anther tissue structure via light microscopy.

steps.description: Sectioning of developing anthers and toluidine blue staining were performed as described (Dobritsa et al., 2010).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform sectioning of developing anthers and toluidine blue staining according to the protocol described in Dobritsa et al., 2010
outputs: Stained anther sections on microscope slides
